author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU) / Dr. Priya Menon (GI) / Dr. Sameer Iqbal (IR)
content:
MICU ADMISSION + PROCEDURES NOTE — UPMC
Date: 5/2/2027 (evening)

EVENTS: Massive hematemesis in the ED → massive transfusion protocol → endotracheal intubation for airway protection → MICU.

PROCEDURES:
- Bedside EGD (GI, Dr. Menon): actively bleeding esophageal varices; band ligation attempted but FAILED to achieve durable hemostasis.
- Emergent TIPS (IR, Dr. Iqbal): performed for refractory variceal hemorrhage with resolution of bleeding; patent shunt (Doppler IMG004).

MEDICATIONS STARTED: Ceftriaxone (variceal-bleed / SBP prophylaxis), octreotide infusion, IV pantoprazole. Sedation for intubation (propofol/fentanyl). Lactulose continued given TIPS-associated encephalopathy risk.

STATUS at end of note: Hemorrhage controlled after TIPS; hemodynamically stabilized with resuscitation. Intubated/sedated. Will monitor closely.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Variceal hemorrhage — controlled s/p TIPS; continue octreotide course + ceftriaxone prophylaxis; serial Hgb.
#Decompensated cirrhosis — lactulose for encephalopathy prophylaxis (heightened post-TIPS); monitor mental status.
#Airway — mechanically ventilated; wean sedation as tolerated.
#Involuntary weight loss — defer dedicated workup until stabilized.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Lena Park, MD (MICU). 5/2/2027.
